AuthData.description: A block of JSON describing the resource to create

AuthSiteData.description: A block of JSON describing the resource to create

Data.description: Specify the value for the optional parameters of KTR.

DataGridUpdate.description: Specify the data for the updated data grid. Ensure wrapped in {}

LicenseData.description: A block of JSON describing the license

RecordingDocument.description: JSON containing optional additional settings for this recording.

RuleGroupDocument.description:
A block of JSON containing the rule group information you want to create.  Note there are many more 
properties which you can set to allow you to override parameters of the rules, etc. but the simplest is provided as an 
example.

QueueDefinition.description: A block of JSON that contains information about the contents of the messages in the queue

total.description: How many values match the request?

from.description: What is the 0-based index of the first item returned in the data

to.description: What is the 0-based index of the last item returned in the data

clients.description: The clients that are included in this result

status.description: OK if the request succeeded, otherwise ERROR

healthScoreTimeFrame.description: Time frame for calculating the health score

description.description: Human-readable description of what this permission allows you to do

blockOverage.description: Does this license block overage once quota has been reached?

name.description: The unique name of this role

permissions.description: The names of the permissions that a user will be granted if they have this role

siteId.description: A UUID to uniquely identify this site

name.description: The unique name to identify this user

isConfidential.description: Value is masked, e.g. for passwords/keys characters (true/false)

isEditable.description: Values can be edited by users without GLOBALPARAMETER_CAN_CONFIGURE permission characters (true/false)

isForcedValue.description: Rule parameter values will be overridden characters (true/false)

picklistItems.description: Array of items to select from

lastModifiedDate.description: When was this object last modified? Timestamp in ISO-8601 format

lastModifiedUser.description: What is the name of the user who last modified this object?

defaultCacheDuration.description:
When you add a new table to the cache, this is the default (in seconds) for how long data will say in 
that table if it has not been written recently.

description.description: The description of what this property does

newClient.description: (Optional) name of the client where the copy should be made

removeOriginal.description: remove the original parameter after creating the copy (essentially a move / rename)

TableTypeEnum.description: The type of the Cache table

fields.description: The fields of the table. Will always extend any existing tables

name.description: The name of the table to alter.

isClusteringColumn.description: Is this field something that the data is physically clustered on?

isPartitionKey.description: Is this field part of the partition key?

CacheTableDirectionEnum.description: The direction of the clustering column

cql.description: The insert, select or delete CQL command

retries.description: Number of retries, before giving up

CacheConsistencyLevel.description: The consistency level of a request.

rows.description: The number of rows in the analysis data.

modifiedon.description: The date and time when the modification was made, in a custom format.

modifiedby.description: Identifier of the user who made the modification.

CacheDirectionEnum.description: The direction of the clustering column

description.description: A description of the rule group

enableInvoke.description: If you want to allow this rule group to be invoked, set this to true

name.description: The name of the rule group

referencedRules.description: If you want the rule group to reference rules, provide the list here

public_ip.description: The public ip of the new instance

host.description: The internal ip address of the new instance

version.description: The version number of the instance

attributeName.description: The name of the setting to modify

collectionName.description: The name of the setting collection to modify

clientInPathRequiredForReport.description:
Which client would you like the report for? You must be able to access this client 
to report on it.
clientInPathRequiredForReport.name: client

clientInPathNotRequiredForReport.description:
Which client would you like the report for? You must be able to access this client 
to report on it.
clientInPathNotRequiredForReport.name: client

targetInPathRequired.description: Target - is where rule is published.
targetInPathRequired.name: target

pagingParameterFrom.name: from
pagingParameterFrom.description: The zero-based index of the first item to return.

pagingParameterTo.name: to
pagingParameterTo.description: The zero-based index of the last item to return.

sortProperty.description:
List of comma-separated values, by which the returned rules will be sorted by. The possible values 
are: name, client, description, businessGoal, dataType, isConfidential, isVisible, isEditable, isForcedValue.
sortProperty.name: sortProperty

sortDescending.description:
List of comma-separated true/false values indicating either ascending (false) or descending (true) 
order of each sortProperty
sortDescending.name: sortDescending

sortPropertyTemplate.description:
List of comma-separated values, by which the returned rules will be sorted by. The possible values 
are: templateName, templateLastModifiedDate, templateLastModifiedUser, client, numberOfParameters, numberOfSwitchCases, numberOfDataGrids, numberOfInputs, numberOfOutputs, version, serviceCost
sortPropertyTemplate.name: sortProperty

sortPropertyRuleGroup.description:
List of comma-separated values, by which the returned rules will be sorted by. The possible values 
are: name, client, target, description, modifiedDate, numberOfReferencedRules
sortPropertyRuleGroup.name: sortProperty

searchText.description: Filter results to those which contain this text
searchText.name: searchText

csvFormat.description: If you want CSV, enter csv here
csvFormat.name: format

healthScore.description: Time Frame for calculating the health score of a rule. Allowed values are hour, day, min
healthScore.name: healthScore

includeStatistics.description: Should the results include the execution statistics for each rule? Default true
includeStatistics.name: includeStatistics

createdRuleUrl.description:
The url the rule was published to. Only rule with this url will be returned. In the format of: 
target/client/rule_name
createdRuleUrl.name: createdRuleUrl

parameterName.description: Get the report for a specific parameter
parameterName.name: parameterName

parameterValue.description: Get a list of rules which have a specific parameter value
parameterValue.name: parameterValue

templateFilter.description: Filter to return only rules using specified template
templateFilter.name: templateFilter

includePublic.description:
Flag to indicate whether rules from the public client should be included in the results, please use 
iClients=public instead
includePublic.name: includePublic

dataGridName.description: Name of the Data Grid that will be updated
dataGridName.name: dataGridName

checkTemplateLatestVersion.description: Should a check to verify rule has the latest templates version be performed?
checkTemplateLatestVersion.name: checkTemplateLatestVersion

auditLogEntryMaximum.description: Maximum number of audit log entries to return
auditLogEntryMaximum.name: auditLogEntryMaximum

validate.description: Set to 'True' to only validate the JSON (and not save it)
validate.name: validate

tableKeyInQuery.description: If you want a row of data to be returned, provide the partition key
tableKeyInQuery.name: key

tableKeyInQueryRequired.description: If you want a row of data to be returned, provide the partition key
tableKeyInQueryRequired.name: key

tableKeysInQuery.name: keys
tableKeysInQuery.description: Values for clustering keys. If the table has a composite key, provide the values in order.

tableSample.description: return a sample of n rows from the table
tableSample.name: sample

tablePivot.description: Would you like the data pivoted? (Only for CSV report)
tablePivot.name: pivot

tableFieldValue.description:
If this is a fieldvalue type table, you can ask for a list of keys or values.  If you specify 
fieldvalues, you must also provide a key
tableFieldValue.name: fieldvalue

tableLimit.description:
If you are asking for a list of values from a fieldvalue table, you can request to return only the 
most recent, limited to this number.  If not provided, a system limit will be used.
tableLimit.name: limit

previewInQueryRequired.description:
By default API works in preview mode and returns a list of cache columns and tables that would be 
dropped. Only when preview set to false the columns are actually dropped
previewInQueryRequired.name: preview

allVersions.description: Do we want all versions of the file listed?
allVersions.name: allversions

allDetails.description: Do we want to retrieve details of Data Grids present in this template.
allDetails.name: getAllDetails

basicInfo.description: Only retrieve the basic information about this template, like name, client and url template.
basicInfo.name: basicInfo

targetClient.description: Request template's Data Grids' details with parameter information for this client.
targetClient.name: targetClient

startdate.description:
If you want to filter your request by date range, this is the starting date for the time period, 
please provide in ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ)  All times are UTC.
startdate.name: startdate

enddate.description:
If you want to filter your request by date range, this is the ending date for the time period, please 
provide in ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ)  All times are UTC.
enddate.name: enddate

csvInQuery.description: if true then returns csv file
csvInQuery.name: csv

itemLimitInQuery.description: The maximum number of items to return.
itemLimitInQuery.name: limit

sequenceNumberInQuery.description: Sequence Number to identify rule invoke logs
sequenceNumberInQuery.name: sequenceNumber

templateUrlInQuery.description: Template url, if you want to filter your request by template url
templateUrlInQuery.name: templateUrl

logSourceInQuery.description: Pick the source of the logs. Currently only elastic search is supported
logSourceInQuery.name: source

searchPhraseInQuery.description: The phrase to search for in the logs
searchPhraseInQuery.name: searchPhrase

tileNameInQuery.description: The name of the tile, if you want to filter your request by tile name
tileNameInQuery.name: tileName

tileIdInQuery.description: The id of the tile, if you want to filter your request by tile id
tileIdInQuery.name: tileId

logTypeOrTimeseries.description: What kind of logging do you want to see?
logTypeOrTimeseries.name: type

ruleGroupInPathRequired.description: The name of the rule group
ruleGroupInPathRequired.name: rulegroupname

includePropertiesInQuery.description: Do we want all details of each rule group? (default is true).
includePropertiesInQuery.name: includeproperties

allVersionsInQuery.description: Do we want all versions of the file listed?
allVersionsInQuery.name: allversions

ruleGroupVersionInPathRequired.description: The version of the rule group
ruleGroupVersionInPathRequired.name: version

TargetNodeInQuery.description:
Which instance (identified by host name) do you want to query? Leave blank to pick an instance 
randomly
TargetNodeInQuery.name: targetnode

post.description:
If the message was read with 'deleteAfterRead=false' then you must call this API to remove the message 
from the queue after your code has successfully processed it

parameters.description: The target in which the queue is located
parameters.name: target

parameters.description: The name of queue which contains the message you want to acknowledge
parameters.name: queueName

post.summary: Remove a set of messages from a given queue.

get.description: Return a JSON array containing zero or more messages

parameters.description: Target - the target of where the queue is stored.
parameters.name: target

parameters.description: The name of the queue to read messages from
parameters.name: queueName

parameters.description:
The number of messages to read in this request. Default 1. If messages are bundled together then more 
messages may be returned than requested. If deleteAfterRead is false then the maximum is 10, and you must call `POST 
/queue/{client}/{target}/{queueName}/ack` on the current messages before any more will be available to read
parameters.name: numberOfMessages

parameters.description:
If the queue currently contains fewer than the numberOfMessages requested, how long (in seconds) 
should we wait for new messages to arrive? Default 0
parameters.name: maxWaitTimeInSeconds

parameters.description:
Should the messages being read be automatically removed from the queue? Default true. If this is 
false then the `POST /queue/{client}/{target}/{queueName}/ack` API must be called with the message details once the 
data has been processed
parameters.name: deleteAfterRead

parameters.description: If the queue is locked, you must provide the key in this parameter
parameters.name: lock

get.summary: Gets one or more messages from the queue

post.description: Used for posting messages to a queue. The data passed in the message is not validated in any way

parameters.description: client name
parameters.name: client

parameters.description: Target - the target of where the queue is stored.
parameters.name: target

raw data?

get.description: This will be parsed by the Spoon plugin so that a rule created as an autoloader can be edited
get.summary: Get the autoloader JSON for a specific rule. This is normally used by the Rule plugin within Spoon

post.description:
This will automatically change the structure and the values within the rule so that it can load the data 
required. Tis is normally called by the Rule plugin from within Spoon. Only rules that were created as an autoloader can 
be altered.

requestBody.description: Specify the autoloader JSON for the updated rule. Ensure wrapped in {}

post.summary: Save the JSON for an autoloader rule

post.description: A copy of this rule will be started in the given target.

parameters.description:
The target to copy the rule to - must be different from the current target.  Can optionally be 
provided in the requestBody as JSON instead if preferred
parameters.name: destinationtarget

post.summary: Copy this rule to the given target.

get.description: Information returned is filtered by user permissions.
get.summary: Get information about the data grid for a specific, published rule.

post.description:
If a rule already has an associated data grid, here it is possible to enter new data and change the grid 
contents.  Note, you cannot add or remove columns but you can change their format.  Do a get on the data grid to get the 
existing structure.
post.summary: Enter new data for an exisiting data grid and update

post.description: This is to append new data only. Existing data remains unchanged
post.summary: Add new data grid rows to a rule's Data Grid

get.description: Information returned is filtered by user permissions.
get.summary: Get the rule designer JSON for a specific rule. This is normally used by the Rule designer user interface

post.description:
This will change the structure and the values within the rule, as defined by the rule designer. If the 
new rule structure is not valid and the rule is running then this call will fail unless you are only validating the rule

requestBody.description: Specify the rule designer JSON for the updated rule. Ensure wrapped in {}

post.summary: Save or validate the rule designer JSON for a rule

post.description: this will update the rule with new default parameters.

requestBody.description: key value pairs of param name and value. Ensure wrapped in {}

post.summary: update the default values for parameters.

delete.description:
Cause this rule to go to 'waiting' state.  A rule that is paused will return an error code if it receives 
traffic (i.e. is invoked). Restart the rule to allow it to serve traffic again
delete.summary: Pause this rule

post.description: This will set a new value for a rule's priority weighting

requestBody.description: key value pairs of param name and value. Ensure wrapped in {}

post.summary: Update the rule's priority weighting
